messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following workplace wellbeing app privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"WellWork is provided by your employer to support employee wellbeing. We collect mood check-ins, stress levels, and engagement survey responses. Individual data is never shared with your employer — only anonymised, aggregated reports are provided. You use the app voluntarily. Data is stored in the UK. You can delete your account at any time."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated. Voluntary use implies consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) but this is not named. The employer-provided context complicates freely given consent — this tension is not addressed.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [FAIL] Mood, stress levels, and mental wellbeing data constitute health data under Art. 9. No explicit consent or Art. 9(2) basis is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Employee wellbeing support and anonymised employer reporting are clearly stated and separated.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Mood, stress, and engagement data are proportionate for a wellbeing tool.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [FAIL] No retention period stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only account deletion (erasure) referenced. Other rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] No processors disclosed. The controller/processor relationship between employer and WellWork is not defined.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] UK storage stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Processing of employee health and mental wellbeing data likely requires a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis, consent validity in employment context unaddressed), Art. 9 (mental health data without explicit consent), Art. 5 (no retention period), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 28 (controller/processor relationship undefined), Art. 37 (DPO likely required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State explicit lawful basis — ideally explicit consent (Art. 9(2)(a)) with acknowledgement that it is freely given independently of the employment relationship
- Obtain separate explicit consent for mental health and wellbeing data
- Add a retention period
- Add full data subject rights section
- Define controller/processor relationship between employer and WellWork
- Disclose technology sub-processors
- Appoint and disclose DPO
- Add breach notification commitment
